zh.lifeSummary: 當Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde於1222年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德時，他的父親Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele43歲，母親Alix de Rosoy17歲。他於1245年與Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque結婚。他去世於1293年12月26日享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde于1222年出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德时，他的父亲Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele43岁，母亲Alix de Rosoy17岁。他于1245年与Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque结婚。他去世于1293年12月26日享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde was born in 1222, in Oudenaarde, East Flanders, Belgium, his father, Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele, was 43 and his mother, Alix de Rosoy, was 17. He married Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque in 1245. He died on 26 December 1293, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde est né en 1222, à Audenarde, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Alix de Rosoy, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque en 1245. Il est décédé le 26 décembre 1293, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde 1222 in Oudenaarde, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele 43 und seine Mutter Alix de Rosoy 17. Er hat 1245 Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque geheiratet. Er starb am 26. Dezember 1293 im Alter von 71 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde è nato il nel 1222 nel Oudenaarde, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, il suo padre, Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Alix de Rosoy, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque nel 1245. Morì il 26 dicembre 1293, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Graf Jan I van Oudenaardeが1222年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンオウデナアルドで生まれた時，父Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pameleは43歳，母Alix de Rosoyは17歳であった。1245年にMathilde Mahaut De Crésecqueと結婚した。彼は1293年12月26日に71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde(이)가 1222년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 우데나아르드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix de Rosoy(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1245년 Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1293년 12월 26일 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde nasceu em 1222, em Oudenaarde, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Alix de Rosoy, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque em 1245. Ele faleceu em 26 de dezembro de 1293, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde родился в 1222 г. в местности оуденаард, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Alix de Rosoy, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque в 1245 г. Он умер 26 декабря 1293 г. в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Graf Jan I van Oudenaarde nació en 1222, en Oudenaarde, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Alix de Rosoy, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque en 1245. Murió el 26 de diciembre de 1293, a la edad de 71 años.
